proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=96, 94.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +30.5pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8815 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
Regime concentration on Choppy (0.7925) is just below the 0.8 trigger; Bear-gated cohort activation will not amplify this until regime rotates
UP_TRI dominates open exposure (37/53 ≈ 70%), but only 11 positions are in Bear regime — Bear-gated boost will activate selectively, not against the full UP_TRI book
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
R-multiple 0.61 reflects Day-6 forced-exit dominance (D-8 review pending); if exit logic changes, R-multiple may revise and tier could shift

If regime rotates from Choppy back to Bear (prior 7d showed 3 Bear days), the Bear-gated cohort will activate immediately on top of an already UP_TRI-heavy book — TAKE_SMALL sizing partially mitigates
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=96, wr=94.7%, edge +30.5pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification is robust (n=96, Wilson lower 0.8815, +30.5pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (stable, 7 days), so activation is deferred and the existing UP_TRI/Choppy concentration is not immediately compounded. Consistent with multiple prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-04-29 through 2026-05-01) with no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: UP_TRI × Metal: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, sector=Metal (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +24.0pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.72 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)

Portfolio is heavily UP_TRI-skewed (37/53 open) and Choppy regime concentration is at 0.7925 (just below 0.80 trigger); even a narrowed Metal-specific boost could nudge this further if Metal UP_TRI signals fire in the current regime
R-multiple 0.72 reflects D-8 forced-exit dominance; if Day-6 exit policy is revised, R-multiple could shift and tier should be re-evaluated
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +24.0pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification is robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +24.0pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) and promotion is correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.72. The sector=Metal filter narrows rather than amplifies the existing UP_TRI overweight (37 of 53 open), and TAKE_SMALL sizing limits incremental concentration. Consistent with multiple prior approvals of this exact cohort and no trader rejection on record.
